methodsAttempted.query: site:sussex.ac.uk generative AI policy students assessment University of Sussex
methodsAttempted.notes: Search was used for discovery only; evidence came from official Sussex staff, student, and about pages.
Fetched official AI in education hub pages on academic integrity, AI tools, assessment, and talking with students.
Fetched official student misconduct guidance and an AI safety advisory; the advisory was rejected as news-only for claims.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found University of Sussex staff AI assessment guidance, AI tools/data guidance, talking-with-students guidance, AI principles, and student academic misconduct guidance. A student news advisory was official but rejected as news-only.

artifacts.claimText: University of Sussex staff guidance says module convenors determine and communicate AI-use permissions via module Canvas sites and choose one of three assessment-level permissions: AI use prohibited, AI in an assistive role, or AI with an integral role.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Sussex staff guidance provides a prohibited-use assessment statement saying generative AI tools must not be used to generate materials or content for that assessment, while allowing other assistive technology for registered reasonable adjustments.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Sussex student misconduct guidance includes unauthorized or inappropriate use of digital technologies including AI, and gives examples including AI use where prohibited and submitting permitted AI-generated work without required acknowledgement.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Sussex staff guidance says staff and students can access a data-protected Microsoft Copilot with Sussex credentials, and says university data such as learning and teaching content should be used in Copilot rather than less protected AI tools.

artifacts.claimText: University of Sussex AI principles say decisions on whether AI use is permitted, not permitted, optional, or required in learning or assessment will be made explicit.

artifacts.claimText: University of Sussex staff guidance suggests telling students that AI detection tools are fallible and cannot be relied upon.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow teaching/academic-integrity communication claim; the source frames this as staff guidance for discussion with students, not a binding detector policy.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: It is up to module convenors to determine and communicate AI use permissions via module Canvas sites. For each assessment, choose one of three permitted levels of AI use: AI use is prohibited; AI can be used in an assistive role; AI has an integral role.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Permissions for AI use in assessment at Sussex

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Generative AI tools must not be used to generate any materials or content for this assessment. The purpose and format of this assessment makes it inappropriate or impractical for AI tools to be used. Students registered with the Disability Advice team and in receipt of reasonable adjustments are still permitted to use other assistive technology as required.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Assessment level AI permission statements, Statement 1

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Misuse of digital technologies includes artificial intelligence. Examples include: using AI or other digital tools, such as translation tools in an assessment where their use has been prohibited; submitting AI-generated work, where this is permitted, without required acknowledgment.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Academic misconduct, Misuse of digital technologies

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Being logged into Copilot with your Sussex account means that your data is protected. Your chat results won't saved or made available to Microsoft, meaning any data isn't passed outside of the organisation. This is in contrast to both the free version of Copilot and other AI tools which may not be protecting your data. If you are using university data in an AI tool, such as learning and teaching content, then ensure you use Copilot.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Acknowledge that AI detection tools already exist, many much more sophisticated ones are in development and, predictably, a web-based sub-culture of ways to fool the detection systems is also growing. Explain that all are fallible and cannot be relied upon.

artifacts.title: University of Sussex AI Policy Staged Findings
artifacts.summary: Staged review found official Sussex guidance on assessment-level AI permissions, prohibited-use wording, AI-related academic misconduct examples, protected Copilot use for university data, institutional AI principles, and staff guidance for discussing AI detection limitations. Claims remain staged candidates and are not canonical public policy claims.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Codex CLI retry hit an account usage limit, so this fallback artifact was generated from official sources already fetched during the batch and revalidated locally.
No login-gated, paywalled, CAPTCHA-protected, or non-official sources were used.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
